findings.label: FI-038
findings.description: useSessionLayout crash risk — NOT reproducible. LayoutProvider wraps all routes at app.tsx:121. useLayout() throws only if called outside <LayoutProvider>, which cannot happen in production. useSessionLayout always produces valid tabs/view objects via createMemo. No guard needed.

findings.label: FI-039
findings.description: Context usage display null crash — NOT reproducible. sync.data is always defined (getter returning child store). Null-safety fully handled: sync.data.message[params.id] ?? [] (line 39), sync.data?.part ?? {} (line 52), context()?.usage ?? 0 (line 47), formatter().number(undefined) returns '—' (ctx-format.ts:6). Previous sessions already applied null-safety fixes.
findings.notes: Line 52 uses sync.data?.part with unnecessary optional chaining on sync.data — inconsistent with the rest of the codebase which accesses sync.data.message directly (no ?.). Harmless but inconsistent.

findings.label: FI-040
findings.description: Tooltip IS present (line 130: <Tooltip value={tooltipValue()}>). Shows tokens, usage %, cost, and compaction status. aria-label set on button (line 142). Gap: tooltip shows raw numbers but doesn't EXPLAIN the percentage — e.g., 'Context window: X% full' vs just displaying 'X%'. Consider adding a brief explanation in the tooltip.

findings.label: FI-041
findings.description: Context update after session switch — NOT reproducible. SolidJS createMemo reactively tracks params.id. When route changes, messages() recomputes, metrics() recomputes via getSessionContextMetrics, usage() recomputes. No stale data — the reactive chain updates synchronously on param change.
findings.notes: layout.tabs(sessionKey) and layout.view(sessionKey) are createMemo'd in session-layout.ts:17-18, so they also react to sessionKey changes correctly.

findings.label: FI-042
findings.description: Layout context provider — present and sound. LayoutProvider wraps all routes at app.tsx:121 inside AppShellProviders. createSimpleContext helper throws 'Layout context must be used within a context provider' if accessed outside <LayoutProvider>. No additional guard needed at the provider boundary.

entry_points: useSessionLayout — session-layout.ts:11, used by session-context-usage.tsx, session-side-panel.tsx, use-session-commands.tsx, session-context-tab.tsx
SessionContextUsage — session-context-usage.tsx:32, the context gauge component
SessionContextTab — session-context-tab.tsx:93, the full context tab panel

recommended_architect_scope.summary: All 5 FI items have been assessed. Only CTX-003 (tooltip explanatory content gap) is actionable. The other four are either already handled by the architecture, already fixed in prior sessions, or non-issues. Recommended work: add explanatory text to the context gauge tooltip describing what the percentage represents (context window fill level).

actionable_items.description: Enhance tooltip to explain 'X% — context window fill level' rather than just displaying the percentage

non_actionable.reason: useSessionLayout cannot crash — LayoutProvider wraps all routes, createSimpleContext guards the boundary
Null-safety already fully handled with ?? and optional chaining; prior sessions fixed the edge cases
SolidJS reactive memos ensure prompt update on session switch
LayoutProvider correctly mounted at app root with built-in throw-on-missing guard
